response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and tooling experience with a focus on testability and modern development practices, but his background lacks direct embedded firmware, C/Linux kernel, or network security experience required for this role. His diverse skill set and open-source contributions show adaptability, but the role's specific technical demands (e.g., MACsec, 802.1X, switch chip knowledge) are not clearly addressed in his resume.
strengths: Extensive software development experience (10+ years) across multiple domains, including embedded-adjacent projects (e.g., hardware control at Altom Consulting).
Strong open-source contributions and tooling expertise (e.g., Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner), demonstrating problem-solving and collaboration skills.
Fluent English and proven ability to work in cross-functional teams (e.g., hardware/software collaboration at National Instruments).
concerns: No explicit experience with C programming for embedded systems or Linux kernel modification, which are core requirements.
Lacks demonstrated expertise in network security technologies (e.g., MACsec, 802.1X) or Layer-2/3 networking for switches.
Japanese language proficiency (native-level required) is absent, and no evidence of prior work in Japan or with Japanese teams.
